title: Motherwort tincture
subtitle: A cardiac nerve-calming herb herbal tincture for palpitations
excerpt: Dried motherwort aerial parts soaked in 40% vodka for 4-6 weeks. Used in western herbal practice for mild, stress-related palpitations and nervous heart sensations in an otherwise healthy reader. Not for use in pregnancy.
sourceNotes: Maud Grieve (the early 20th-century botanical writer), A Modern Herbal, motherwort entry: the cardiac nerve-calming herb and uterine tonic tradition. King's American Dispensatory (Felter & Lloyd, 1898), Leonurus cardiaca, this spirit extraction preparation and cardiac-nervous use. the herbal reference on Leonuri cardiacae herba, the traditional use for nervous cardiac symptoms and mild anxiety.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Macerate 4-6 weeks in a cool dark cupboard. Strain and bottle. Keeps 2 years in amber glass.

projectSchedule.title: Pack the jar and cover with vodka
projectSchedule.body: Pack dried motherwort into the soaking jar. Pour over vodka to cover by 2 cm. Seal, label, and shake. Place in a cool dark cupboard.

projectSchedule.title: Strain and bottle
projectSchedule.body: Strain through muslin, pressing the herb firmly. Bottle in amber glass. Deep olive-green is the expected colour.

glossaryTerms.definition: A herb with both nervine (calming) and cardiac-toning properties. Motherwort is the classic cardiac nervine of the western tradition, used for palpitations that have an identifiable anxiety or stress component.

content.text: Motherwort (Leonurus cardiaca) is the western herbal tradition's primary
. It is used for a racing or anxious heart in an otherwise healthy reader. Western herbalists use it for stress-related heart symptoms and for rapid heartbeat during the menopause.

attrs.title: Important safety notes
attrs.body:
Not medical advice. Consult a qualified herbalist or doctor for ongoing or serious symptoms.

Medical clearance first. Palpitations that have not been evaluated by a doctor should be assessed before starting any herbal treatment. Palpitations can be benign or can reflect cardiac arrhythmias that need specific treatment. This herbal tincture is for palpitations that have already been medically assessed as benign.

Cardiac medication. Motherwort potentiates digoxin, beta-blockers, and antiarrhythmics. Do not combine with any cardiac medication without consulting your prescriber.

Pregnancy. Do not use motherwort in pregnancy. It has well-documented uterine-stimulating and uterine-stimulating properties and is avoided throughout pregnancy.

Breastfeeding. Use with caution during breastfeeding. The alkaloids can pass into breastmilk; consult a herbalist before use.

Palpitations that need immediate medical attention. Chest pain with palpitations, breathlessness with palpitations, dizziness or loss of consciousness with palpitations, or palpitations that are new, severe, or rapidly increasing need urgent medical assessment. Do not delay seeking medical care to try herbal treatment.

content.text: Pack dried motherwort loosely into a 500 ml soaking jar. Pour over the vodka to cover the herb by at least 2 cm. Seal and label with the herb, spirit strength, and date. Shake daily. At 4-6 weeks, strain through muslin folded double, pressing the spent herb firmly. Bottle in amber dropper bottles. This spirit extraction should be deep olive-green and smell distinctly bitter and herbal. Label with the herb, date, and ratio (1:5, 40% spirit). Keeps 2 years.

Adults: 2-4 ml in water, up to three times a day. Take for up to 4 weeks continuously; take a 1-week break before another course. Take only after palpitations have been medically assessed. Paediatric use: not for children under twelve.
Amber glass bottles, cool and dark. Keeps 2 years from the bottling date.
When not to use this remedy
In pregnancy. On cardiac medication. Before palpitations have been medically assessed. Seek urgent care for palpitations with chest pain, breathlessness, or dizziness.
